companies.name: CoreWeave
companies.narrative: A GPU owner-operator running an essentially fully-contracted (~98% take-or-pay), hyperscaler-anchored book — Microsoft ~67% of revenue, plus Meta (~$21B), OpenAI (~$22B) and Anthropic, against a ~$99B backlog at ~5-yr weighted term. Near-term capacity is pre-sold and capacity-constrained, so the investable question isn’t whether it earns but what the back-half pipeline re-signs at, how fast it diversifies off Microsoft, and whether ~$23B of debt and $31–35B of FY26 capex can be carried while margins are still near breakeven. The target leans meaningfully on the path to 8 GW by 2030 — roughly a fifth of it rests on rumored capacity.
bull: ~98% take-or-pay book and ~$99B backlog de-risk near-term cash flows
Hyperscaler anchors (Microsoft, Meta, Anthropic) plus capacity-constrained demand
Cost of debt falling — first IG-rated GPU-backed DDTL (~6%) vs ~9% legacy
Diversifying off Microsoft as the backlog lengthens toward 7 years
bear: ~$23B net debt plus $31–35B FY26 capex — refinancing and execution risk
Microsoft 67% concentration and large weak-credit OpenAI exposure (~$22B)
Adjusted operating margin ~1% — profitability unproven at scale
Leased-heavy rent drag; Denton construction delays already drew litigation
catalysts: New anchor signings and the 8 GW-by-2030 pipeline converting to contracts
Self-builds (Kenilworth, Lancaster, Hammond) coming online — owned economics
Further IG-rated, lower-cost financings cutting the blended debt rate
Energization pulling Helios and Ellendale capacity active
risks: Counterparty concentration (Microsoft) and OpenAI credit quality
Refinancing and rate risk on ~$23B of debt; capex outrunning cash
Power-cost inflation in PJM (Lancaster, Chicago) and constrained NJ
AI-demand or GPU-rate softening hitting re-contracting

log.x: Chicago-area lease at Applied Digital (Polaris Forge Bldg 3), 15-yr ~$2.2B.
Closed $3.1B DDTL 5.0 — first syndicated HPC-backed financing.
Q1'26 revenue $2.08B (+112% YoY); backlog $99.4B; FY26 capex guide raised to $31–35B.
Meta agreement expanded to ~$21B through 2032.
Closed $8.5B DDTL 4.0 — first investment-grade GPU-backed facility.

basis.plannedRaise: ~10-12% equity slice of $31-35B/yr FY26 capex; ~84% DDTL-debt funded, equity opportunistic (NVIDIA/Jane Street)

companies.thesis: Model credits the full 8 GW pipeline at owner economics while the market mainly prices the contracted ~$99B backlog — the gap closes as back-half capacity re-signs. Brakes: ~$23B debt and ~67% Microsoft concentration.

companies.name: Nebius
companies.narrative: A net-cash, prepayment-funded owner-operator that has flipped from spot-tilted scaler into a high-contracted, hyperscaler-anchored buildout. A ~$50B backlog (~92% Microsoft + Meta on ~5-year take-or-pay terms) sits over a smaller, fast-repricing enterprise tail — so ~half of realized revenue is enterprise (high revenue per MW, sub-1-yr), while ~80% of the contracted MW is locked to hyperscalers. Capital is structurally lighter than debt-heavy peers: ~$9.3B cash, low-coupon convertibles, ~$4.8B of customer prepayments funding ~90% of capex, plus a stake portfolio (ClickHouse ~$4.2B, Avride, Toloka, TripleTen) worth value outside the compute sites. The story is execution: only ~170 MW connected today against GW-scale owned campuses (Independence MO, Pennsylvania) that don't energize until late 2027+. Scale is modeled on its growth trajectory, not its (quieter) headline ambitions.
bull: Net cash plus ~$4.8B customer prepayments fund ~90% of capex — far less dilution/leverage risk than debt-heavy peers
~$50B backlog (~92% Microsoft + Meta, ~5yr take-or-pay) gives top-tier-credit, multi-year visibility
>75% of contracted power is owned (cheap Nordic / self-powered gas) — a structural cost-of-power edge
Hidden optionality in the stake portfolio (~$4.2B ClickHouse alone; IPO eyed ~2027) on top of compute
bear: Overwhelmingly a future-MW story: ~170 MW connected vs GW-scale targets — heavy back-end execution risk
Vineland NJ (the Microsoft site) has an NJDEP permit on hold and is barely live — key near-term risk
Thin net-cash cushion (~$848M) flips to net debt as the ~$20-25B 2026 capex deploys
Convertibles defer cash interest but carry equity dilution; Nvidia warrants + converts lift the share count
catalysts: Connected-power step-up through H2 2026 toward the 800MW-1GW year-end target
Microsoft (Vineland) ramping H2 2026; Meta firm compute from early 2027
Independence MO and Pennsylvania campus first-power (late 2027) proving the owned-GW model
ClickHouse IPO / stake monetization (Avride, Toloka) crystallizing the legacy value
risks: Permitting / interconnect slippage at owned campuses (Vineland NJDEP hold; gigawatt power timelines)
Capital intensity: the net-cash buffer erodes into a ~$20-25B capex plan, raising future financing needs
GPU-rate compression hits the fast-repricing enterprise tail and the discretionary $15B slice of the Meta deal
Stake marks are mostly undisclosed private valuations (only ClickHouse is primary-sourced)

log.x: Revenue $399M (+684% YoY); AI-cloud ARR $1.92B; FY26 revenue guide $3.0-3.4B; contracted power raised to >3.5GW, targeting >4GW by YE26; FY26 capex $20-25B.
Announced second US gigawatt-scale owned campus in Pennsylvania (up to 1.2GW, first power ~end-2027).
Broke ground on 400-acre Independence MO campus (up to ~1.1GW, self-powered gas; Phase 1 ~250MW ~Oct 2027).
Nvidia $2.0B prefunded-warrant placement (~8% stake); $4.34B convertible notes priced (1.25% / 2.625%).
ClickHouse raised $400M Series D at $15B post-money; Nebius's ~28% stake marked to ~$4.2B ($781M Q1 revaluation gain).

basis.plannedRaise: NVIDIA $2B + ~$2.5B ATM + 1-2 follow-ons; prepayments fund ~60% of capex

companies.thesis: Roughly fair — the 80%-contracted hyperscaler book and $5.8B of non-core stakes are largely priced in, leaving modest upside from the 6.5 GW ramp. Net-cash balance sheet is the cushion.

companies.name: Riot Platforms
companies.finMix: 0.75% converts + BTC-backed Coinbase loan + ATM/BTC recycling; net debt (net cash on BTC look-through)
companies.narrative: A Bitcoin miner mid-pivot into AI/HPC colocation landlording on ~2.0 GW of owned, approved, cheap-ERCOT power (Corsicana ~1.0 GW + Rockdale ~0.7 GW in Texas; Kentucky mining-only). The pivot is real but early-stage: one signed AI lease — AMD, 50 MW critical IT at Rockdale, a 10-yr deal (~$510M NOI over the initial term) with options for up to ~150 MW more. Everything else is pipeline — Corsicana's first 168 MW core-and-shell is under construction with no signed tenant, inside a ~1 GW envelope that is otherwise uncontracted. Value is dominated by two non-core legacy assets (legacyEV): a 15,680 BTC treasury marked to the live BTC price (so the target moves ~$0.4/sh per ±$10K of BTC) and an FCF-negative 42.5 EH/s mining fleet whose underlying power and land are the very assets being converted to AI. Activist Starboard is pushing the pivot harder. The core question is execution: can Corsicana sign credible tenants and lease up on the implied timeline, or is the AI optionality already more than priced in?
bull: Massive owned, approved, low-cost ERCOT power runway (~1.7 GW Texas) — the scarce input for AI colo, years to replicate
AMD (investment-grade) proof-of-concept lease: 50 MW, 10-yr, ~$510M NOI, ~$1M+ NOI/MW — plus options for ~150 MW more
Optionality-rich balance sheet: ~$0.9B BTC treasury plus mining power/land being recycled into higher-value AI colo
Starboard Value activist pressure pushing faster dealmaking and consolidation/takeover optionality
bear: AI book is one tenant, one site, one building — ~50 MW firm; Corsicana (the bigger prize) has zero signed tenants
Heavy BTC reliance: ~$0.9B treasury and most debt BTC-collateralized; a drawdown hits asset value, collateral and mining cash at once
Legacy mining deeply FCF-negative at spot (cost to mine ~127% of production) — a multi-year J-curve before AI offsets the drag
Speculative core-and-shell capex committed ahead of demand; if Corsicana doesn't convert, large MW sits empty
catalysts: First Corsicana tenant / LOI — currently zero; the single biggest re-rating event (mgmt in talks for the full site)
Corsicana Building 1 (168 MW) core-and-shell completion (~Q2 2027) and AMD Rockdale phases energizing
AMD exercising its ~150 MW expansion options (would roughly quadruple contracted AI MW)
Starboard-driven strategic action — accelerated leasing, a capital partner/JV, or M&A
risks: BTC price — the dominant swing factor on legacyEV, Coinbase collateral coverage, and mining cash flow
Single-tenant concentration: 100% of the contracted AI book is AMD
Speculative-build lease-up risk — large Corsicana capex with no signed demand
Financing / dilution: continued ATM issuance and BTC recycling to fund the build

log.x: $400M permit filed for a new ~168 MW Corsicana core-and-shell (Project Ditto) — speculative build, no tenant.
AMD exercised +25 MW (to 50 MW); Data Center rev $33.2M (~97% one-time fit-out); recurring lease rent only ~$0.9M.
$200M Coinbase BTC-backed term loan refixed to a fixed rate, maturity extended.
Activist (~3.1%) urges faster AI dealmaking, citing AMD as proof-of-concept.
Signs AMD anchor lease (25→50 MW) at Rockdale; acquires 200 acres fee-simple for $96M (funded by BTC sale).

basis.plannedRaise: $0 ATM draws Q1'26; funds via BTC treasury + cash flow + nonrecourse project debt (Starboard anti-dilution)
basis.tier: IG — investment-grade AMD 50MW 10yr lease anchors the colo book

companies.thesis: Owned, powered ~1 GW Corsicana (IG-re-rated on the AMD lease) is credited, but it's ~95% unleased so the market discounts the lease-up. Re-rates as Corsicana signs tenants.

companies.name: Bitdeer
companies.finMix: Convertibles + ATM equity + BTC-collateralized loans; net debt
companies.narrative: A Bitcoin miner and ASIC designer pivoting into AI/HPC by converting power-rich crypto campuses to colocation, leasing capacity to AI tenants on landlord economics, alongside a small high-margin owner-operated GPU-cloud sleeve. The structural edge is owned cheap-to-mid power (Nordic hydro at Tydal, Columbia hydro at Wenatchee, ERCOT at Rockdale, TVA at Knoxville) and an unusually low ~6% blended cost of debt from a ~$1.4B cheap-convertible stack. Its flagship Tydal (Norway) campus has a signed colocation lease that is not yet effective — tenant unnamed, terms withheld. Beyond ~$69M of AI-cloud ARR, essentially nothing is firmly contracted: the GW-scale value drivers (Tydal, Clarington, Niles, Rockdale) are signed-but-conditional or in negotiation, so most modeled MW is owned and scheduled (high existence) but not yet leased. The question is execution — energizing the pipeline, clearing the Clarington litigation, and signing credible AI tenants while financing-funded cash burn and dilution continue.
bull: Owned cheap-to-mid-power campuses convertible to AI colocation — an RIOT-style structural edge
Unusually low ~6% cost of debt from a ~$1.4B cheap-convertible stack (vs miner peers at 9-11%)
Tydal colocation lease signed (flagship proof point); AI-cloud ARR scaling ~6x in four months to ~$69M
Vertically integrated SEALMINER ASIC business (A4 at 9.45 J/TH, US Reno assembly) as a real legacy asset
bear: Near-zero firmly contracted AI demand — the GW-scale colocation pipeline is uncontracted
Tydal lease signed but not yet effective; tenant, term and value undisclosed, contingent on the counterparty
Financing-funded build (~$347M cash used in ops in Q1'26) and shares up ~38% YoY — ongoing dilution
Self-mining gross income negative (−$34.4M Q1'26); legacyEV is BTC-cycle-levered and SEALMINER sales throttled
catalysts: Tydal colocation lease becoming effective with disclosed tenant, term and pricing (~July 2026)
Anchor hyperscaler / neocloud signings at Clarington or Niles converting rumored MW to disclosed
Clarington litigation resolved, unlocking the 570 MW block's timeline
First-power energization milestones across the H2'26 sites (Tydal ph1, Knoxville, Wenatchee)
risks: AI colocation leases fail to materialize and pipeline MW never gets contracted
Clarington litigation drags, indefinitely stalling the largest single block
Continued cash burn forces further dilutive equity or pricier related-party debt
BTC downcycle further impairs self-mining cash flow — the funding source for the AI pivot

log.x: Tydal Data Center AS executes Tydal, Norway colocation lease; not yet effective, tenant/terms undisclosed, contingent on conditions precedent.
AI-cloud ARR ~$69M; 4,248 GPUs deployed (~90% utilization); 171 BTC held (near-zero treasury).
Neighbor lawsuit stalls Clarington 570 MW; timeline to be updated.
Borrowings ~$1.92B (mostly convertibles); cash $261M; self-mining gross income −$34.4M; net debt ~$1.66B.

basis.plannedRaise: ATM cut to $700M, converts preferred; Tydal lease → project debt
basis.equityDiscount: Jihan Wu ~75% voting control + foreign-private-issuer + controlled-company + index exclusion

companies.thesis: Cheap against its ~1.4 GW spec pipeline, but upside is gated by a 15% governance discount (Jihan Wu control) and a near-zero contracted book. Tydal becoming an effective lease is the catalyst.

companies.name: IREN
companies.finMix: ATM equity ($6B program) + low-coupon converts + $3.65B IG GPU financing + Microsoft prepayment
companies.narrative: A former Bitcoin miner (Iris Energy) that has made a genuine owner-operator pivot into AI cloud — the rare name here that OWNS and runs its own GPUs (NVIDIA GB300/B300, ~$5.8B Dell buy, ~150k GPUs installed or on order) rather than leasing space to tenants. It sells GPU-as-a-service from data centers it owns on cheap power: Childress TX (750MW campus, ~3c/kWh ERCOT) and 100% hydro British Columbia. The book is anchored by two top-tier 5-year managed-cloud contracts — Microsoft (~$9.7B, 200MW across Childress Horizon 1-4, 20% prepaid) and NVIDIA (~$3.4B, plus a $2.1B equity right) — ~$3.1B ARR contracted against a ~$3.7-4.4B target, though live AI revenue is still small and the backlog is overwhelmingly future. The swing factor is a ~5.8 GW secured-power pipeline that dwarfs today's live base — Sweetwater TX (~2 GW) and Kiowa OK (1.6 GW), plus first international sites in Spain (Nostrum) and Australia (Bundey) — almost all uncontracted, so the book reads spot-tilted and the value hinges on lease-up. Most capacity operating today is still legacy Bitcoin mining being converted to AI. Legacy/non-core is small — a cash-positive but shrinking mining fleet, the Mirantis AI-cloud software stack (acquired in stock), and a ZERO Bitcoin treasury (mined coins sold daily), so no BTC-price bet. The crux: funding the ~5.8 GW build needs tens of billions on a small share base, so the model now charges that dilution explicitly (uncontracted capacity is raised as equity) — the per-share target is POST-dilution. The real question is whether IREN can contract and commission its pipeline faster than the equity raises dilute it.
bull: Rare owner-operator AI cloud with two blue-chip 5yr contracts (Microsoft ~$9.7B + NVIDIA ~$3.4B) — ~$3.1B ARR contracted, near-zero counterparty risk, 20% Microsoft prepayment
Massive owned, cheap, secured power (Childress 750MW + Sweetwater ~2GW + BC 160MW hydro) — the scarce AI input, mostly freehold
NVIDIA is a strategic partner and equity holder (right to 30M sh @ $70), aligning the GPU supplier to IREN's ramp
Low legacy/BTC risk: zero Bitcoin treasury (sells as mined) and a cash-positive mining fleet — value is core AI, not a BTC bet
bear: Backlog is overwhelmingly future: live AI-cloud revenue only ~$34M/qtr; even the ~$3.1B contracted ARR isn't revenue until GPUs commission
The GW-scale prize (Sweetwater ~2GW) is largely uncontracted — power secured, but tenants/utilization are not; big spec capex ahead of demand
Heavy dilution to fund a ~$20B+ buildout: $6B ATM headroom + ~$6.7B converts + rising GPU debt — the headline target is pre-dilution
Neocloud GPU rental rates down 60-75% from peak; uncontracted GPUs exposed to spot pricing and obsolescence
catalysts: Childress Horizon 1-4 (200MW, Microsoft) commissioning through YE2026 — converting contracted ARR into live revenue
First large Sweetwater anchor contract — the biggest re-rating event for the uncontracted GW pipeline
NVIDIA Blackwell commissioning (early 2027, ARR target to $4.4B) and NVIDIA exercising its $2.1B equity right
Hitting the ~$3.7B YE2026 ARR / 150k-GPU milestone (vs ~74k deployed)
risks: Lease-up risk on the uncontracted Sweetwater GW — large spec capex with cloud demand TBD
GPU economics & obsolescence — spot rate decline, utilization, depreciation on owned GPUs (an owner risk colos don't carry)
Financing / dilution — continued ATM issuance and convert dilution to fund the buildout
Customer concentration — contracted ARR dominated by Microsoft + NVIDIA; a delay or renegotiation at one hits the book

log.x: Closed $3.65B investment-grade GPU financing (Fitch A, ~6%), secured on GPUs + Microsoft cash flows; funds ~96% of the $5.8B Dell GPU purchase with the prepayment.
Signed ~$3.4B 5yr NVIDIA managed-services AI-cloud contract (60MW Childress, ramp early 2027); lifts ARR target to ~$4.4B.
Closed upsized ~$3.0B convertible notes (1.0% due 2033); total convertible principal ~$6.7B.
AI-cloud revenue $33.6M (+94% q/q); ~$3.1B ARR contracted, ~$3.7B YE26 target; Sweetwater 1 substation energized; cash $2.6B.
Secured ~$9.7B 5yr Microsoft GPU-cloud contract (GB300, 200MW across Childress Horizon 1-4, 20% prepay ~$1.9B); paired with the ~$5.8B Dell GPU buy.

basis.plannedRaise: ~$4.9B undrawn of $6B ATM + future programs; MSFT prepay + $3.65B GPU debt + converts carry ~90%

companies.thesis: Most undervalued and most speculative — the model credits a ~5.8 GW mostly-uncontracted global pipeline riding the rising-rate trend, while the market prices today's small live AI book. Post-realistic-dilution but pre-execution: it must build, fill and fund the pipeline.

companies.name: Soluna Holdings

basis.plannedRaise: $500M ATM (H.C. Wainwright) + warrants; sub-$2 micro-cap funds buildout ~entirely by dilution → share count 3-4x

companies.narrative: Soluna is a renewable-powered colocation landlord, not a GPU cloud: it builds wind/solar-co-located data-center shells in Texas (plus a Kentucky site), hosts third-party Bitcoin miners who bring their own ASICs, and is pivoting the same powered-land model to AI/HPC. Today ~123 MW is operating (all Bitcoin hosting/prop-mining) and 83 MW (Kati 1) is energizing/under construction; the AI story rests on Project Kati 2 (Metrobloks JV, 350+ MW roadmap where Soluna contributes site/power/electrical and Metrobloks leads leasing to neocloud tenants) and Project Dorothy 3 (300+ MW, now backstopped by the April 2026 $53M Briscoe Wind Farm purchase giving owned generation and a power cost edge). The whole AI book is pre-tenant — a vertically integrated, cheap-power optionality bet on a tiny (~$220M), heavily diluting, going-concern-flagged balance sheet.
bull: Vertically integrated cheap renewable power — owns the 150 MW Briscoe Wind Farm behind Dorothy, eliminating PPA reliance and creating a durable sub-4c/kWh cost edge for AI/HPC leasing
Real, scarce powered land in ERCOT with a 4.3 GW interconnection pipeline; Kati 2 (Metrobloks JV, 350+ MW) and Dorothy 3 (300+ MW) have site/power secured and the AI pivot is genuinely underway
Operating Bitcoin hosting book growing fast (data-hosting revenue +178% YoY in Q1'26) funds the platform while the AI campuses are built
Near net-cash today (~$41M net cash post-Briscoe) on a ~$220M market cap — large MW pipeline relative to enterprise value if even one AI campus leases up
bear: Zero signed AI/HPC tenants — the entire AI thesis is pre-lease; Kati 2 leasing is outsourced to JV partner Metrobloks and only ‘formal negotiations with at least one potential tenant’ have started
Severe dilution: shares +627% YoY to ~158M, a $500M ATM shelf open, warrants to Generate/YA — the model values the buildout on today’s shares but the equity to fund it isn’t subtracted
Going-concern stress: ~$79M LTM net loss, negative FCF, Altman Z ~-2.57, and ~$34M of 9% Series A preferred dividend arrears ahead of the common
Sub-scale and capital-starved versus AI-colo peers (Applied Digital, Crusoe, the larger miners) — needs huge third-party project capital that may price punitively or not arrive
catalysts: First signed AI/HPC anchor lease at Kati 2 or Dorothy 3 — the single biggest re-rating event; currently zero tenants
Kati 2 project-level financing close (top-tier AI-infra bank engaged) that funds the build without crushing common dilution
Kati 1 full energization + Dorothy 1B prop-mining consolidation lifting hosting EBITDA and proving execution
Dorothy 3 interconnection / power milestones converting the Briscoe-owned generation into shovel-ready AI capacity
risks: AI leasing never materializes and the spec shells stay Bitcoin-only at far lower revenue-per-MW
Equity/warrant dilution and a punitive ATM draw at sub-$2 swamp any per-share upside; Nasdaq listing/compliance risk
Preferred dividend arrears (~$34M) and project-level minority partners (Spring Lane, Navitas, Generate, Galaxy) siphon value before the common
Bitcoin price/difficulty downturn dries up the hosting cash flow that currently funds the platform mid-pivot
companies.finMix: Near net-cash (~$41M post-Briscoe): ~$28M project/equipment debt (Generate, Galaxy, Green Cloud, equipment) + ~$2M finance leases vs ~$69M cash; funded chiefly by equity ($500M ATM, registered directs, warrants) and project-level capital from Spring Lane/Generate; no BTC treasury.
companies.thesis: A distressed micro-cap option: ~280 MW of mostly pre-tenant AI capacity on cheap renewable power, nearly offset by the ~3-4x dilution needed to fund it. Binary on landing a JV/anchor before the going-concern clock.

companies.name: Galaxy Digital

basis.plannedRaise: Helios is debt-led: $1.4B project financing facility at 80% loan-to-cost + $2.6B corporate cash/stablecoins + financial-services cash flow; Galaxy repurchased $65M of Class A stock in Q1 2026 rather than issuing. ~$1.5B is a realistic incremental equity contribution to fund the uncontracted multi-GW expansion phases beyond project-debt capacity.
basis.equityDiscount: Dual-class structure (191.8M Class A vs 198.4M supervoting Class B), founder/Novogratz control, controlled-company governance -> 0.10.

companies.narrative: Galaxy is a crypto financial-services franchise (trading, asset management ~$5B AUM + $3.2B staked, lending, ~$650M venture book) being repriced as an AI-infrastructure landlord on the strength of its Helios campus in Dickens County, west Texas. Helios is a converted Bitcoin-mining site leased to CoreWeave: 526 MW of critical IT load contracted across three phases (Phase I 133 MW delivered Apr 2026; Phase II 260 MW H1 2027; Phase III 133 MW) on a 15-year take-or-pay lease expected to generate >$1B average annual revenue at ~90% lease-level EBITDA margin. ERCOT has approved 1.6+ GW total (potential to ~2.5-3.5 GW), so most of the campus is approved-but-uncontracted optionality on top of the signed CoreWeave book.
bull: 526 MW critical IT load contracted to CoreWeave on a 15-year take-or-pay lease, >$1B avg annual revenue at ~90% lease-level EBITDA margin -- a long-duration, high-margin annuity.
1.6+ GW ERCOT-approved (campus potential ~2.5-3.5 GW) on owned freehold land in a cheap-power ERCOT/west-Texas basin -- scarce, energizable expansion optionality beyond the contracted book.
Funded with $1.4B project debt at 80% LTC plus $2.6B corporate cash/stablecoins and a cash-generative financial-services arm -- limited equity dilution; company is buying back stock.
Optionality of a second anchor or expanded CoreWeave deal across the ~1.1 GW of approved-but-uncontracted capacity could re-rate the campus.
bear: Single-tenant concentration: nearly the entire AI book is CoreWeave, itself a levered, unrated AI-cloud whose own demand/financing is the key dependency.
Most of the 1.6+ GW is approved but uncontracted -- value beyond 526 MW depends on leasing capacity that has no tenant yet.
Legacy crypto financial-services and treasury earnings are volatile and BTC/market-sensitive (Q1 2026 net loss $216M); segment value can swing materially.
Dual-class founder control and heavy notes-payable load ($3.1B) add governance and leverage risk during a multi-GW capex cycle.
catalysts: Phase I rent commencement and full 133 MW handover to CoreWeave (Q2 2026), then Phase II deliveries H1 2027.
A second anchor tenant or expanded CoreWeave lease across the ~1.1 GW approved-but-uncontracted capacity.
Energization milestones on the 830 MW ERCOT-approved expansion and progress toward the ~2.5-3.5 GW campus potential.
Investability re-rating as contracted lease cash flows season (potential tier lift toward IG/REIT).
risks: CoreWeave counterparty/financing stress would hit the entire contracted book given single-tenant concentration.
Construction, transformer/interconnection delays push out Phase II/III energization and the uncontracted expansion.
Crypto-market drawdown depresses the financial-services/treasury legacy value and overall liquidity.
Funding gap on the multi-GW buildout forces more debt or equity than modeled, increasing leverage/dilution.
companies.finMix: Debt-led: $1.4B Helios project facility (80% LTC, 36mo) + $3.1B total notes payable (incl. ~$2.1B exchangeable notes) + $2.6B corporate cash/stablecoins + financial-services cash flow; buying back stock, minimal equity issuance.
companies.thesis: Fair — the 15-yr CoreWeave Helios lease (526 MW) is priced in; the ~1.6 GW unleased ERCOT pipeline is the option you're buying. Upside needs the next anchor; the financial-services arm is ballast.

companies.name: Hut 8 Corp.

basis.plannedRaise: parent recourse net debt ~$0 (Coatue convert nets w/ cash); $7.5B is nonrecourse SPV project debt netted against the campuses
basis.stake: 55.3% economic of American Bitcoin (ABTC), marked live; replaces the old generic legacy residual (HUT folded ~all mining into ABTC)

companies.narrative: Hut 8 has pivoted decisively from BTC miner to power-first AI data-center LANDLORD: it builds greenfield, owned, NVIDIA-DSX-spec shells and signs 15-year triple-net leases to tenants who bring their own GPUs. Two anchor leases are signed — River Bend (245 MW IT, Louisiana, leased to Fluidstack with a Google payment backstop under a multi-GW Anthropic partnership) and Beacon Point Ph1 (352 MW IT, Texas, to an undisclosed high-IG tenant) — totaling ~597 MW contracted and ~$16.8B base-term value (~$1.1B stabilized annual NOI). Both are funded with IG non-recourse project bonds ($3.25B + $4.25B), keeping the parent low-recourse, while the legacy mining business is carved into separately-listed, ~80%-owned American Bitcoin (ABTC).
bull: Two signed 15-yr triple-net leases (~597 MW, ~$16.8B base-term value, ~$1.1B stabilized NOI) anchored by a Google payment backstop and an IG tenant — durable, contracted, infrastructure-like cash flow
Non-recourse IG project financing ($3.25B River Bend + $4.25B Beacon Point at ~95% LTC) funds the buildout with minimal parent recourse and little equity dilution — capital-efficient growth
8.4 GW development pipeline plus ROFO on +1 GW at River Bend gives a long runway to compound contracted MW; Anthropic relationship can scale to ~2.3 GW
Owned freehold land with secured utility interconnects (AEP Texas 1 GW, Entergy Louisiana) in cheap-power regions — the scarce input (power) is locked
bear: Tenant concentration and credit: River Bend rests on Fluidstack (a thin-balance-sheet neocloud) with reliance on the Google backstop; Beacon Point's IG tenant is undisclosed — counterparty quality is partly a leap of faith
Heavy project leverage (~$7.5B notes) front-loads risk before any cash flow; first energization is not until Q1–Q2 2027, so execution/construction risk is live
Equity already prices a huge re-rating — ~$13B market cap on assets that are mostly pre-revenue shells; multiple compression if the AI-lease land grab cools
Complex entity structure (~80% ABTC sub, BTC treasury, JV notes) obscures the clean landlord story and invites a sum-of-parts discount
catalysts: Initial energization / first data-hall delivery at Beacon Point (Q1 2027) and River Bend (Q2 2027) converting backlog to cash NOI
Commercialization of Beacon Point Phase 2 (700 MW utility available Q1 2027) and additional pipeline leases signed with named IG/hyperscaler tenants
Coatue $150M convertible mandatory redemption/conversion (Q2 2026) removing the last parent-recourse debt
Conversion of any pipeline GW (e.g. River Bend ROFO to 1 GW, Anthropic scaling toward 2.3 GW) into signed take-or-pay leases
risks: Construction delays, cost overruns, or interconnect slippage pushing energization past 2027 and straining project-bond covenants
Fluidstack default with a contested or capped Google backstop, or the undisclosed Beacon Point tenant proving weaker than 'high-IG' framing
AI-capacity overbuild / GPU-rate deflation softening renewal economics and pipeline lease pricing beyond the signed base terms
BTC price collapse impairing the ~5,600-BTC treasury and the ABTC stake, hitting parent liquidity and the legacy value
companies.finMix: Funded mainly by IG non-recourse project bonds (~$7.5B, 6.1–6.2% coupons, 95% LTC, fully amortizing) plus capital recycling; parent recourse limited to a $200M BTC-backed FalconX facility and the in-the-money Coatue $150M convert; ~5,600 BTC + ~$700M cash and an ~80% ABTC stake provide a liquidity cushion.
companies.thesis: Overvalued on the math: campuses credited at full value but funded ~95% by $7.5B of nonrecourse project debt, leaving thin equity, plus a live ~55% ABTC stake. The market pays for developer optionality the leverage doesn't support.

companies.name: Cipher Digital

basis.plannedRaise: ~$600M ATM registered + per-project equity slices (~$118M Fluidstack expansion; mgmt says it can fund the equity component of the 3rd campus); buildout is ~85% nonrecourse project-bond + convertible funded, so equity is a thin top-up — modeled ~$1.2B cumulative
basis.tier: IG — direct AWS 15-yr 300MW lease (~$5.5B) plus Google-backstopped Fluidstack book; ~$11.4B contracted revenue anchored by investment-grade counterparties

companies.narrative: A former Bitcoin miner (Cipher Mining, rebranded Cipher Digital in early 2026) executing one of the cleaner BTC-to-AI landlord pivots: it builds powered data-center shells on owned, cheap-ERCOT Texas land and leases them to hyperscale tenants who bring their own compute. Unlike most peers it has already signed a genuinely investment-grade book — AWS direct (300 MW gross / 216 MW critical IT, 15-yr, ~$5.5B at Black Pearl) and Fluidstack at Barber Lake (207 MW critical IT, 10-yr, Google backstopping ~$333M of obligations), plus a third undisclosed IG-hyperscaler campus — totaling ~$11.4B contracted revenue and ~$787M average annualized NOI. The capital stack is mostly nonrecourse project debt (a $2.0B Black Pearl bond at 6.125%, a $1.7B Cipher Compute/Barber Lake note at 7.125%) plus low-coupon converts, leaving only ~$960M net debt and ~$4.2B cash. The story is execution and lease-up: ~700 MW of HPC is contracted and energizing through late 2026, but the GW-scale prize (McLennan, Mikeska, Colchis, Ulysses) — toward a ~4.2 GW 2030+ ambition — is owned and scheduled but largely uncontracted.
bull: Genuinely investment-grade contracted book already signed — AWS direct (15-yr, ~$5.5B) plus Google-backstopped Fluidstack — ~$11.4B contracted revenue, ~$787M avg NOI, rare among BTC-to-AI landlords
Owned, low-cost ERCOT power and land across multiple Texas campuses (Barber Lake, Black Pearl, McLennan, Mikeska, Colchis) — the scarce AI input, mostly freehold and years to replicate
Capital-light per-share story: ~$960M net debt on ~$4.2B cash; buildout funded mostly by nonrecourse project bonds + low-coupon converts, with thin equity top-ups
AWS de-risked construction (covers overruns above $9.5M/IT-MW) and is winding down the BTC treasury (1,116 BTC, full divestment targeted by YE2026), cutting crypto beta
bear: Most of the ~4.2 GW ambition is uncontracted: only ~700 MW of HPC is leased; McLennan/Mikeska/Colchis/Ulysses are owned and scheduled but tenantless spec build
Concentration: the signed book is effectively AWS + Fluidstack/Google across two Texas sites — a tenant delay or renegotiation hits a large slice
Legacy mining (Odessa 207 MW, 11.6 EH/s) is being wound down with negligible going-concern value, and the pivot still consumes cash (Q1'26 adj. EBITDA ~-$48M, net loss $114M)
Heavy nonrecourse project leverage ($5.2B total debt) ahead of full lease-up; pipeline capex committed before demand is signed
catalysts: Barber Lake (Fluidstack/Google) and Black Pearl (AWS) energizing and rent commencing through H2 2026 — converting contracted revenue into live NOI
First anchor tenant at McLennan / Mikeska / Colchis — the single biggest re-rating event for the uncontracted GW pipeline
Disclosure of the third IG-hyperscaler campus (tenant, MW, term) and any 1 GW JV crystallizing
Ulysses (Ohio/PJM, interconnect-approved) energizing in 2027 and AWS/Fluidstack expansion options being exercised
risks: Lease-up risk on the uncontracted GW pipeline — large spec capex with demand TBD
Tenant concentration (AWS + Fluidstack/Google) and counterparty/renegotiation risk on the signed book
Execution/energization slippage and construction cost inflation across multiple simultaneous TX builds
Refinancing and dilution risk on $5.2B nonrecourse project debt plus converts ahead of full lease-up
companies.finMix: Nonrecourse project bonds (Black Pearl $2.0B @6.125%, Barber Lake $1.7B @7.125%) + converts ($1.3B 0% + $173M 1.75%) + $200M revolver + ATM equity; ~$960M net debt
companies.thesis: Undervalued — 700 MW of signed hyperscaler colo plus a ~2 GW queued pipeline credited near-full while the market still treats it as a miner. Re-rates as Barber Lake energizes and the pipeline contracts.

companies.name: MARA Holdings

basis.plannedRaise: ~$1.5B: $2B ATM facility established Mar 2026, ~$2.5B equity raised across 2024-25; BTC sales + Barclays bridge + ~$785M assumed Long Ridge debt carry most buildout capex, but a spec-heavy multi-GW pivot with no signed AI leases will lean on the ATM at the margin.

companies.narrative: MARA Holdings is a top-tier Bitcoin miner (~72 EH/s, ~1.9 GW of power across 19 sites, 35,303 BTC) repositioning its power-rich, largely-owned portfolio into an AI/HPC landlord — building powered data-center shells (Long Ridge OH 505MW gas plant + up to 600MW DC; MPLX West Texas 400MW→1.5GW; a Starwood JV targeting ~1GW near-term to >2.5GW) to lease to investment-grade AI/IT tenants while flexing workloads back to mining when economics favor it. The investable question is execution and contracting: the AI book has zero signed leases today (only "inbound interest"), buildout starts in 2027 with first power around mid-2028, and most of the multi-GW pipeline is announced rather than secured. Capital structure is heavy — ~$2.3B converts post-Q1 buyback plus the ~$1.5B Long Ridge bridge — partly offset by a still-large but shrinking BTC treasury that management is now selling to cut debt.
bull: Owns ~1.9 GW of power and large freehold land/fuel/interconnect — scarce, ownable optionality for AI shells
Long Ridge (505MW gas, 600MW DC) and MPLX West Texas (to 1.5GW) give a credible multi-GW owned trajectory in cheap power regions
Starwood JV brings a real-estate developer's tenant-sourcing, design and capital to de-risk the landlord pivot
Flex design lets sites toggle mining↔AI, putting a mining floor under otherwise-uncontracted MW
bear: Zero signed AI/HPC leases — only inbound interest; the entire landlord thesis is still un-contracted
~$2.3B converts + ~$1.5B Long Ridge bridge against a shrinking BTC treasury and thin operating cash
Most of the multi-GW pipeline is announced/LOI, not secured — heavy rumored provenance and 2028+ energization
First-mover-late: a BTC miner with no data-center-leasing track record competing against proven neoclouds/landlords
catalysts: First signed IG AI/HPC anchor lease at Long Ridge or a Starwood site
Long Ridge acquisition close (H2 2026) and FID/groundbreaking on the 200MW DC (1H 2027)
MPLX West Texas definitive agreements and FID converting the LOI to construction
Further BTC-funded debt paydown lowering the convert balance and net debt
risks: Execution risk on building/leasing data centers — a new competency vs. its mining core
BTC price drop simultaneously hits treasury value, mining cash flow, and the financing plan
Refinancing/dilution risk if ATM is leaned on harder than telegraphed to fund the buildout
AI demand or GPU-rate softening leaving owned MW stranded on mining-only economics
companies.finMix: BTC-treasury sales + zero/low-coupon converts (~$2.3B) + Barclays bridge & assumed debt on Long Ridge (~$1.5B) + $2B ATM at the margin; Starwood JV co-finances projects.
companies.thesis: Looks cheap because the model credits ~2.5 GW of owned power, but nearly all is mining/spec with zero signed AI leases (real AI-leasable ~200 MW). Needs an actual AI conversion, not just owned megawatts.

companies.name: Core Scientific

basis.plannedRaise: ~$600M: buildout is overwhelmingly debt- and tenant-funded. Closed a $3.3B 7.75% senior secured notes offering (May 2026), used partly to repay the $1.0B 364-day term loan; CoreWeave prepaid ~$750M of fit-out (~$1.5M/MW); ~$2B FY26 capex covered by notes proceeds + BTC liquidation. Equity issuance is therefore modest (top-up ATM for expansion land/power), not a primary funding lever.
basis.equityDiscount: 0.05: 10-Q/A restatement disclosed a material weakness in ICFR (assets-for-demolition impairment), disclosure controls deemed ineffective for 2024-2025; small governance/reporting-quality discount. No founder supervoting (clean one-share-one-vote, which is why the CoreWeave merger could be voted down).
basis.tier: proven: single-tenant (CoreWeave ~100% of HPC colocation revenue) take-or-pay book; not yet diversified IG income despite long term and strong anchor.

companies.narrative: Core Scientific is a landlord: it builds powered data-center shells and leases them to CoreWeave, which brings its own GPUs (colocation), under a 12-year, $10B+ take-or-pay book (~$850M average annual rent, ~590 MW across Denton, Dalton, Muskogee, Marble and Austin, ~80-85% facility margin). The CoreWeave all-stock acquisition collapsed in October 2025 when shareholders rejected it as undervalued, so CORZ stays independent and is racing to convert a ~1.9 GW gross / 1.3 GW leasable footprint plus Pecos and Muskogee 1.5 GW expansions into a multi-GW colocation platform. Legacy self-mining is in deliberate runoff (mining assets written down ~$266M, substantially all BTC being sold in 2026 to fund the AI build), so the investable question is how fast it signs a second anchor tenant beyond CoreWeave and energizes the pipeline against a ~$4.4B debt stack.
bull: 12-yr, $10B+ CoreWeave take-or-pay book (~$850M/yr, ~590 MW) energizing to full delivery by early 2027 with ~80-85% facility margins
Owned freehold campuses on cheap ERCOT/stranded power with secured interconnects; CoreWeave prepaid ~$750M of fit-out, de-risking capex
Large secured power runway (Pecos and Muskogee toward ~1.5 GW each, Hunt County ~285 MW) gives years of build optionality if a second tenant signs
Independent again after the rejected CoreWeave bid; $3.3B notes and BTC liquidation fund the near-term build without heavy equity dilution
bear: Single-tenant concentration: CoreWeave is ~100% of HPC colocation revenue and no second anchor has signed since emergence
~$4.4B gross debt ($3.3B 7.75% notes + ~$1.1B converts) against a still loss-making P&L; expansion capacity beyond CoreWeave is uncontracted
Material weakness in internal controls (10-Q/A restatement) and large warrant/convert overhang dilute equity (~505M fully diluted vs ~318M basic)
Mining wind-down destroys legacy cash flow before colocation fully ramps; execution and energization timing risk on a multi-GW pipeline
catalysts: Signing a second anchor/hyperscaler colocation tenant for the ~700+ MW of uncontracted secured leasable capacity
Full CoreWeave 590 MW delivery by early 2027 and Dalton/Muskogee commissioning completing through 2026
Muskogee 330 MW acquisition regulatory clearance (expected Q3 2026) and Pecos 1.5 GW power-procurement milestones
Investment-grade-style financing or REIT-conversion path if the contracted book diversifies; completion of BTC monetization
risks: CoreWeave counterparty/credit risk and reliance on its capacity-constrained demand; any CoreWeave stress hits ~100% of HPC revenue
Refinancing/rate risk on $3.3B 7.75% notes and converts; capex outrunning cash if a second tenant is slow to sign
Speculative pipeline (Pecos/Muskogee 1.5 GW) may not energize on schedule or at assumed power cost; ERCOT congestion
Governance/controls remediation and warrant-driven dilution; AI-demand or GPU-rate softening pressuring re-leasing economics
companies.finMix: $3.3B 7.75% senior secured notes due 2031 + ~$1.1B convertibles (2029/2031) + finance leases; CoreWeave fit-out prepayment (~$750M) and BTC liquidation fund capex; minimal equity reliance.
companies.thesis: Undervalued vs its CoreWeave colo book (~590 MW, 12-yr) plus ~1.3 GW of pipeline; the market discounts the single-tenant concentration. The CoreWeave relationship is both the value and the risk.

companies.name: TeraWulf

basis.plannedRaise: Buildout is overwhelmingly debt/Google-backstopped (~$3.2B Google backstop, $3.2B WULF Compute senior secured notes, $2.5B converts); equity issuance is incremental ATM + acquisition stock (KY/MD deals) plus Google warrants for 32.5M shares, so realistic cumulative equity raise is modest vs ~$13.8B cap — est. ~$600M through 2028.

companies.narrative: TeraWulf is a Bitcoin miner that has pivoted decisively into AI/HPC colocation landlord — it builds powered data-center shells at Lake Mariner (NY) and Abernathy (TX) and leases them to tenants (Core42, Fluidstack) who bring their own GPUs, with Google backstopping ~$3.2B of Fluidstack's lease obligations and holding ~14% pro forma. In Q1 2026 HPC lease revenue ($21M) surpassed mining for the first time, and the company carries a large contracted book (~$12.8B incl. extensions) plus a ~2.2 GW owned, mostly-uncontracted pipeline (Muskie KY 1GW+, Lake Hawkeye, Chesapeake). The thesis is a re-rate from miner to long-duration power/colocation landlord, financed by debt rather than dilution.
bull: Hyperscaler-grade validation: Google ~$3.2B backstop + ~14% equity stake de-risks Fluidstack lease credit and lowers project financing cost
Large signed, long-dated book — Lake Mariner ~360MW (Core42 + Fluidstack 10yr) plus Abernathy 168MW (25yr) = ~$12.8B contracted incl. extensions
Owned freehold land + secured power across ~2.8 GW portfolio (incl. 1GW+ Muskie KY in cheap-power region) gives durable optionality at low implied $/MW
Financing carried by debt/backstops, not equity — limited dilution relative to scale; HPC revenue already exceeds mining
bear: ~$2.7B net debt and a capital-intensive buildout; revenue ramp is back-end-loaded (most contracted MW energize H2-2026, Muskie not until 2028-2030)
Heavy customer concentration in Fluidstack — a thinly-capitalized neocloud whose lease credit leans on Google's backstop rather than its own balance sheet
~2.2 GW of pipeline (Muskie Ph II, Lake Hawkeye, Chesapeake) is uncontracted and partly speculative; leasing risk on the back half of the stack
Legacy Bitcoin mining is being wound down/impaired; BTC treasury (13,099 BTC) injects price volatility and quarterly mark-to-market noise
catalysts: CB-3/CB-4/CB-5 energization and rent commencement through H2-2026 (Fluidstack ~300MW going live)
Abernathy TX JV delivery in Q4-2026 (168MW, 25-yr lease)
New anchor lease(s) for the Muskie Kentucky 1GW+ campus or Lake Hawkeye/Chesapeake pipeline
Project-debt financings closing under Google backstop; potential further Fluidstack/Google capacity expansions
risks: Fluidstack counterparty/credit risk if AI-cloud demand softens or Google support is not extended to new buildings
Construction/energization delays or cost overruns pushing back the back-end-loaded ramp
Rising rates or tighter debt markets raising the cost of the debt-heavy financing model
Power/interconnection or permitting delays at Kentucky, Lake Hawkeye and Chesapeake pipeline sites
companies.finMix: Debt-led: ~$2.5B converts + $3.2B WULF Compute senior secured notes + $0.1B KY bridge, ~$3.1B cash; Google ~$3.2B backstop; minimal ATM/equity reliance.
companies.thesis: Slightly rich — Lake Mariner/Abernathy AI leases (Fluidstack/Google-backstopped) are credited, but you're paying for brownfield that's mostly 2028-30 and uncontracted. Near-term contracted MW set the floor.

companies.name: Fermi Inc (Fermi America)

sites.n: Matador — gas Phase 1 (turbines ordered, 6GW air permit)

basis.plannedRaise: ~$3.5B: pre-revenue, multi-GW gas+shell capex vs ~$243M cash; ~$785M non-recourse equipment finance funds turbines, equity/ATM funds the rest
basis.tier: proven — no contracted income; re-rates only on a signed hyperscaler/OpenAI anchor

companies.narrative: Fermi Inc / Fermi America (NASDAQ:FRMI) — 'Project Matador,' a 5,236-acre powered-land play in Amarillo TX (99-yr Texas Tech lease, next to DOE Pantex). The case is OPTIONALITY ON A SCARCE, MASSIVE POWER SITE: a ~6 GW TCEQ air permit APPROVED (the binding scarce asset), a 10-yr Energy Transfer firm gas supply (~2 GW), ~1.5-2.2 GW of gas turbines ordered/LOI, and a 200 MW grid ESA — a credible ~2 GW firm secured-power base, with an 11-17 GW permitting ambition (nuclear AP1000 / SMR / solar) as a deep out-of-the-money option. But it is PRE-TENANT and in governance reset: the first anchor (Amazon-linked) collapsed Dec 2025, the CEO and CFO departed into a 'Fermi 2.0' restructuring, and talks (including OpenAI) are unsigned. Value here credits the ~2 GW firm secured power as graded RAW-power optionality (estimated/rumored, pre-tenant), not built or contracted capacity — a signed hyperscaler/OpenAI anchor is the binary re-rating event.
bull: ~6 GW TCEQ air permit APPROVED + firm Energy Transfer gas (~2GW) + 1.5-2.2GW turbines ordered — a scarce, near-irreplaceable powered-land position
Behind-the-meter gas self-generation bypasses the multi-year ERCOT/SPP interconnection queue
Free option on the 11-17 GW ambition (AP1000 nuclear COLA accepted) if it ever develops
A signed hyperscaler / OpenAI anchor would re-rate the entire site overnight
bear: ZERO binding tenants — first anchor collapsed Dec 2025; OpenAI/hyperscaler talks unsigned
Governance/execution reset: CEO + CFO departed, 'Fermi 2.0', securities class action
Multi-billion capex vs ~$243M cash → heavy dilution to build anything beyond the firm ~2GW
11-17 GW headline is permitting ambition, not secured capacity; nuclear is 2032+ and likely slips
catalysts: First binding hyperscaler / OpenAI anchor lease — the single re-rating event
Gas turbines energizing (~2 GW gas+grid targeted end-2027)
New permanent CEO / 'Fermi 2.0' execution
2nd ~5 GW air-permit approval (toward the 17 GW envelope)
risks: No tenant ever signs — the site stays raw, unleased power
Heavy dilution to fund the multi-GW build
Nuclear/SMR timeline slips or never funds
Governance + litigation overhang
companies.finMix: Near net-cash today (~$421M debt incl. equipment finance vs ~$243M cash → ~$178M net debt, op-leases excluded); funded so far by IPO (~$746M), converts/preferred, and non-recourse equipment financing (MUFG $500M, Yorkville $156M) — but the multi-GW buildout will be overwhelmingly equity/ATM-dependent given no contracted cash flow.
companies.thesis: The firm ~2 GW of secured gas power (approved 6 GW air permit, turbines ordered) roughly supports the price; the premium is the 11-17 GW ambition + OpenAI-anchor optionality, credited at ~zero. A signed anchor is the binary re-rate — no tenant yet.

companies.name: WhiteFiber, Inc.

basis.plannedRaise: Buildout is debt/convert-led, not equity-led: $230M 4.5% convertible notes (Jan 2026, $25.91 strike), a $100M (expandable to $150M) Bit Digital delayed-draw term loan ring-fenced for NC-1 Phase 1, a CAD60M RBC facility, and a $20M Iceland term loan carry most capex. ~$150M assumes only a modest post-IPO ATM to top up equity given the controlled-company structure.
basis.tier: Proven, not IG: anchor tenants Nscale and Cerebras are venture-backed neoclouds, not investment-grade rated; no REIT structure.
basis.equityDiscount: 0.10 controlled-company discount: Bit Digital (BTBT) owns ~71.5% post-IPO and is also a related-party lender, limiting minority-shareholder control.

companies.narrative: WhiteFiber is Bit Digital's carved-out AI-infrastructure arm (IPO Aug 2025, BTBT still ~71.5%), running a hybrid model: an owner-operated GPU cloud (~1,200 H100/H200/B200/GB200 GPUs) plus a fast-growing colocation/landlord business that leases powered shells to tenants who bring their own GPUs. The landlord side now dominates the growth story — NC-1 in Madison NC anchors a 10-year, ~$865M, 40MW Nscale colocation deal (billing Q2-Q3 2026) and MTL-3 hosts a 5MW Cerebras contract. The cloud book is shakier: the initial customer (~71% of 2025 revenue) paused pending renegotiation and DNA Fund terminated, leaving WhiteFiber leaning on colo take-or-pay and a large unbuilt NC-1/Montreal pipeline funded mostly with convert and related-party debt.
bull: NC-1 Nscale deal is a marquee anchor: 10yr, ~$865M, 40MW with 3% escalators and a path to double by end-2027 — durable colo cash flow on owned, low-cost Duke Energy power (54MW already delivered)
Vertically integrated and capital-efficient: management cites $8-10M/MW retrofit cost vs ~$13M industry, ~6-month build timelines, and owns NC-1, MTL-2, MTL-3 freehold
Buildout largely pre-funded with non-dilutive capital ($230M convert, $100-150M Bit Digital DDTL, RBC + Iceland TLs), limiting near-term equity dilution
Optionality on NC-1 to 99MW (4yr) and potentially 200MW, plus a ~1.5GW pipeline under review and Boosteroid 50k-server ROFR
bear: GPU cloud book is fragile: largest customer (~71% of 2025 rev) paused pending renegotiation; DNA Fund terminated with $7.3M receivables outstanding — owner-side revenue quality is poor
Counterparties are sub-IG neoclouds (Nscale, Cerebras, Boosteroid), not hyperscalers — concentration and credit risk on the contracted book
Controlled company: Bit Digital owns ~71.5% and is a related-party lender, so minorities ride along on BTBT's decisions and capital allocation
Loss-making and capital-hungry: Q1 2026 net loss $(12)M on $21.9M revenue, only $3M adj. EBITDA, $169M Q1 capex against $75.8M cash — heavily reliant on continued financing access
catalysts: NC-1 Nscale Phase 1/2 (40MW) energization and billing ramp through Q2-Q3 2026
Resolution of the paused initial cloud customer renegotiation (re-start or replacement)
Nscale expansion to ~80MW (double by end-2027) and incremental NC-1 fill toward 99MW
Draw-down/upsize of the Bit Digital DDTL and any further colo signings from the ~1.5GW pipeline
risks: Anchor tenant (Nscale) financing/credit wobble would impair the headline $865M book
Power-delivery or construction delays at NC-1 beyond the 99MW/4yr schedule
Refinancing/dilution risk if the 2031 convert moves toward its $25.91 strike or debt markets tighten
Related-party governance: Bit Digital control could subordinate WYFI minority interests in financing or strategic decisions
companies.finMix: $230M 4.5% convert due 2031 (+$120M zero-strike call), $100-150M Bit Digital delayed-draw TL, CAD60M RBC facility, $20M Iceland TL; $75.8M cash; ~38.6M shares; no BTC treasury (stayed with BTBT).
companies.thesis: Undervalued AI hybrid (GPU cloud + colo to Nscale/Cerebras), but a single-tenant, single-site, thin-float bet — concentration is the thesis. Its value also drives BTBT's look-through.

companies.name: HIVE Digital Technologies

sites.n: BUZZ current GPU fleet (H200/A-series + 504 B200; Manitoba/Quebec leased DCs)
Bell Merritt, BC — 2,304 GB200 (Cohere/Bell sovereign AI)
Grand Falls / New Brunswick Tier III+ upgrade (25k+ GPUs)
Yguazú, Paraguay Tier III GPU cloud (hydro, future)

basis.plannedRaise: HIVE funds the AI buildout heavily off ATM equity — in FY26 it issued 53.5M shares for $119.2M (Amended Oct-2024 ATM) plus ~$56.9M (Nov-2025 ATM), ~76% share growth YoY, on top of a $115M 0% exchangeable note (Apr-2026) for the GB200 procurement; cumulative forward ATM ~$250M is realistic for a spec-heavy small-cap with active distribution agreements (KBW/Stifel/Canaccord).
basis.equityDiscount: 0.1: chair/CEO Frank Holmes influence, foreign private issuer (Canada/Bermuda note structure), serial ATM dilution and very high beta (~3.7).
basis.tier: proven: AI book is early-stage neocloud ARR with 6–24 month terms and a not-yet-investment-grade anchor (Cohere); no IG-rated contracted income.

companies.narrative: HIVE is a Bitcoin miner (~24.5 EH/s across ~464 MW of mostly-owned hydro capacity in Paraguay, Canada and Sweden) pivoting into AI through subsidiary BUZZ HPC — an owner-operator "sovereign" GPU cloud that procures and runs NVIDIA GPUs and sells compute, not a colocation landlord. The AI book is still small (~$35M realized ARR on ~5,500 GPUs, $19.5M FY26 HPC revenue) but ramping fast: a $220M / 3-yr GB200 deal with Bell and Cohere (2,304 Grace-Blackwell GPUs at Merritt, BC, live late-2026 / early-2027) adds ~$70M ARR, and a Tier III+ upgrade of the owned 70 MW New Brunswick site is pitched to hold 25,000+ GPUs. The investable question is execution and funding: BUZZ is a sub-scale neocloud competing with far larger owner-operators, contracts are short (6–24 months) against a non-IG anchor, and the buildout leans on serial ATM dilution plus a 0% convert while the legacy mining business runs a thin BTC treasury (just ~150 coins after spending 2,139 BTC on equipment).
bull: Owner-operator GPU cloud with real, fast-growing ARR ($35M realized, $100M+ contracted, $200M+ targeted by end-2026)
Sovereign-AI positioning + Bell national fiber/DC partnership and Cohere anchor give Canadian distribution moat
Cheap hydro power (Itaipú/Paraguay, Quebec, Sweden) — low-cost, renewable energization for both mining and AI
$115M 0% exchangeable note funds GB200 capex with minimal cash interest and capped-call dilution protection (cap $4.92)
bear: Sub-scale neocloud (~5,500 GPUs) competing against CoreWeave/IREN-class operators with far deeper hyperscaler relationships
Short AI contract terms (6–24 mo) against a non-IG anchor (Cohere) — low revenue durability vs take-or-pay peers
Heavy serial ATM dilution (~76% share growth YoY) and high beta (~3.7); equity is the primary funding source
Most disclosed MW is still BTC mining; AI footprint is small and much of the GPU-cloud pipeline is rumored/early
catalysts: GB200 cluster at Bell Merritt energizing late-2026/early-2027 and the +$70M ARR converting to billed revenue
GPU fleet doubling to ~11,000 and AI Cloud ARR crossing the $200M target
Grand Falls / New Brunswick Tier III+ conversion securing a hyperscaler or large enterprise tenant
TSX uplisting (May 2026) plus any move toward longer-term / higher-credit AI contracts
risks: AI execution/ramp risk — delays or under-utilization on GB200/Tier III+ buildouts
Funding risk: reliance on ATM equity and converts; further dilution if AI ARR lags capex
GPU-rate and AI-demand softening hitting short-term, on-demand and re-contracting revenue
Bitcoin price / mining economics drag on cash flow and the thin (~150 BTC) treasury; counterparty concentration on Bell/Cohere
companies.finMix: $115M 0% exchangeable notes (2031) + serial ATM equity + Dell finance lease (504 B200s) + mortgage/term loans; AI capex funded mainly by equity and the convert, not contracted prepayments.
companies.thesis: Modest upside — a small live GPU-cloud (~9 MW) plus a 320 MW GTA pipeline on owned green power, still mostly a BTC miner. Cheap optionality on the buildout.

companies.name: Applied Digital

sites.n: Polaris Forge 2 Bldg 1 (Harwood ND, IG hyperscaler)
Polaris Forge 1 ELN-03 (Ellendale ND, CoreWeave; completes 400 MW)
Polaris Forge 2 Bldg 2 (Harwood ND; completes 200 MW)
Delta Forge 1 Ph1 (Rapides Parish LA, IG hyperscaler)
Polaris Forge 3 Ph1 (northern US, IG hyperscaler)
Delta Forge 1 Ph2 (LA; completes 300 MW lease)
Delta Forge 1 expansion (430 MW campus balance, uncontracted)

basis.plannedRaise: ~$1B judgmental: APLD funds buildout via $2.15B PF2 senior secured notes (6.75% '31), Macquarie $5B perpetual preferred ($2.25M/MW, 12.75%) + ~$900M NCI + converts; telegraphs minimal common-equity/ATM issuance
basis.tier: IG — ~1.2 GW under 15-yr take-or-pay, ~65% of revenue backed by U.S. investment-grade hyperscalers (Polaris Forge 2/3, Delta Forge 1); CoreWeave book de-risked to A3 via springing guarantee

companies.narrative: A pure-play AI data-center landlord that exited its own GPU cloud (spun into ChronoScale, ~97%-owned) precisely so hyperscalers would stop seeing it as a competitor — now leasing purpose-built powered shells under ~15-year take-or-pay leases. It has crossed ~1.2 GW of contracted capacity across four campuses (~$31B base / up to ~$73B with renewals): Ellendale/Polaris Forge 1 (400 MW, CoreWeave, now A3-guaranteed) plus Polaris Forge 2, Polaris Forge 3 and Delta Forge 1 leased to U.S. investment-grade hyperscalers (~65% of revenue IG). The investable question is execution and energization on a ~$3B-per-campus buildout funded largely by project debt, Macquarie's perpetual preferred and NCI rather than common equity — plus whether the planned REIT conversion lands.
bull: ~1.2 GW fully contracted under 15-yr take-or-pay; ~$31B base backlog, ~65% IG-hyperscaler-backed
Cloud-business exit removes the channel conflict that blocked colo leasing — pure landlord/REIT story
Financing model leans on project debt + Macquarie's $5B perpetual preferred + NCI, sparing common dilution
CoreWeave book de-risked to A3 via springing guarantee; new tenants are direct IG hyperscalers (Oracle-class)
bear: Heavy execution/energization risk delivering ~$3B-per-campus shells across four sites by 2027
12.75% Macquarie preferred and 6.75% notes are expensive capital that sits ahead of common
Single-tenant-per-campus concentration; one IG hyperscaler anchors both Polaris Forge 3 and Delta Forge 1
Still loss-making at the corp level; REIT conversion not yet achieved and carries structuring risk
catalysts: Energization milestones: ELN-02/03, Polaris Forge 2, then Delta Forge 1 / Polaris Forge 3 (2027)
Formal REIT election / conversion timeline and first dividend
New leases filling Delta Forge 1 balance and the ~1 GW expansion options at PF1/PF2
ChronoScale (EKSO) deal close monetizing/deconsolidating the cloud unit
risks: Construction delays or power-interconnect slippage pushing energization and rent commencement
Refinancing/rate risk on project notes; preferred dividend drag if ramp lags
Tenant concentration — adverse credit event at the shared IG hyperscaler or at CoreWeave
AI-demand or colo-rate softening hitting the uncontracted ~1 GW expansion pipeline
companies.finMix: Project-level senior secured notes (6.75% '31) + Macquarie $5B perpetual preferred (12.75%, 15% common in HPC JV) + NCI contributions + converts; modest corporate equity
companies.thesis: Undervalued — ~1.4 GW of 15-yr triple-net leases to CoreWeave/Oracle (~$36B lease value) credited near-full while the market discounts execution and debt. One of the cleanest contracted-landlord books here.

companies.name: American Bitcoin

basis.plannedRaise: BTC treasury + ~89k-rig self-mining; funds via BTC cash flow + capital markets

companies.narrative: American Bitcoin (NASDAQ:ABTC) — a Bitcoin treasury + self-mining vehicle majority-owned by Hut 8 (55.3% economic / ~80% vote). Value is ~7,200 BTC marked live plus a self-mining fleet; tracked here mainly so HUT's stake marks to a live look-through. Not an AI-infra name. (Figures pre the 1-for-15 reverse split approved Jun 2026.)
bull: Direct BTC-price leverage via treasury + mining
Hut 8 operational backing + scale
bear: No AI/HPC exposure — pure crypto
Trades around BTC NAV; ATM dilution
Reverse split pending
catalysts: BTC price
Treasury accumulation / capital raises
risks: BTC drawdown
Dilution
Concentrated Hut 8 / affiliated control
companies.finMix: BTC treasury + mining cash flow + capital markets
companies.thesis: A BTC treasury/miner (Hut 8-controlled), tracked mainly so HUT marks its stake live; screens near BTC NAV. Not an AI bet — pure Bitcoin and dilution exposure.

companies.name: CleanSpark, Inc.

sites.n: Sandersville, GA — AI conversion (Meta frontrunner; basis-of-design)
Sealy / Austin County, TX — Phase 1 (energization H1'27)
Sealy / Austin County, TX — Phase 2-3 (+40 MW each)
Brazoria County, TX — campus (300 MW load secured)

basis.plannedRaise: ~$300M: mgmt is explicitly anti-dilution ("dilution is not a strategy, discipline is"), $0 ATM in 15 months, and bought back $463M of stock. But ~900 MW of TX AI campuses at $9-11M/MW implies ~$8-9B of capex vs ~$0.8B liquidity — once leases sign, some equity at the margin alongside heavy project debt is realistic.
basis.tier: proven: zero signed AI/HPC leases or LOIs — only basis-of-design talks (Meta frontrunner at Sandersville). No IG-rated contracted income yet, so base case stays Proven; re-rating to IG is upside if an anchor lease lands.

companies.narrative: CleanSpark is a top-tier U.S. Bitcoin miner (~50 EH/s, ~13,470 BTC treasury, 1.8 GW of contracted power across Georgia/Tennessee/Mississippi/Wyoming) executing a deliberate, capital-disciplined pivot into AI/HPC as a powered-shell LANDLORD — not a GPU owner-operator. Management is explicit: it will "build the shell, slot in the reference architecture" per tenant and sign long-term leases with "Grade A credit quality" tenants, owning the real estate and power, not the compute. The pivot is real but entirely pre-contract: the AI book has ZERO signed leases or LOIs as of mid-2026 — only basis-of-design negotiations, with Meta cited as the "clear frontrunner" for the 250 MW Sandersville, GA campus and advanced talks with an investment-grade hyperscaler. The land grab is genuine: two greenfield ERCOT campuses near Houston (Sealy/Austin County 285 MW, energizing ~207 MW H1 2027; Brazoria County 300 MW, expandable to 600 MW, ~late-2027/early-2028), both fee-simple land with secured/approved power, plus the existing energized Sandersville site being converted from mining to AI. Capital structure is now convert-heavy ($1.84B of 0% notes, ~$1.56B net debt excl. BTC) but un-diluted — mgmt repaid all BTC-backed lines and bought back $463M of stock, and intends to fund the ~$9-11M/MW buildout via project/high-yield debt against future IG leases rather than equity. The investable question is pure execution: can CLSK convert "frontrunner" talks into a signed IG anchor lease (the single re-rating event) before the speculative TX capex commits, and is the AI optionality already priced into a stock that has run on Meta headlines?
bull: Owns/controls 1.8 GW of power, fee-simple land and energized infrastructure — the scarce input for AI colo; ~900 MW of greenfield ERCOT (cheap-power) campuses already secured near Houston
Meta is the named "clear frontrunner" for the 250 MW Sandersville site plus advanced talks with an IG hyperscaler — a single signed anchor would re-rate the whole book from mining to IG colo
Genuinely capital-disciplined: $0 ATM in 15 months, $463M buyback (~20% fewer shares), all BTC-backed debt repaid — pivot funded by 0% converts + BTC cash flow, not dilution
Asymmetric optionality: ~13,470 BTC treasury (live-marked) plus 50 EH/s mining cash flow underwrite the buildout while the landlord pivot is free-optioned on top
bear: AI book is 100% pre-contract — zero signed leases or even LOIs; the entire landlord thesis rests on basis-of-design talks that may slip or fall through
Late mover with no data-center-leasing track record, competing against proven landlords (CORZ, APLD, Fermi) for the same hyperscaler demand
Big speculative ERCOT capex looming (~$9-11M/MW × ~900 MW ≈ $8-9B) against ~$0.8B liquidity — a financing gap that could force dilution if leases lag the build
BTC-heavy: treasury, mining cash flow and 100% of current revenue ride Bitcoin; a drawdown hits asset value and the self-funding plan at once (Q2 net loss $378M on BTC mark)
catalysts: First signed AI/HPC anchor lease (Meta at Sandersville the frontrunner) — mgmt guides announcements "within less than a year"; the single biggest re-rating event
Sealy (Austin County) TX energizing ~207 MW in H1 2027 and Brazoria County closing/energizing into late-2027
Conversion of Sandersville from BTC mining to AI tenant occupancy (basis-of-design → definitive lease)
Project/high-yield debt financing secured against an IG lease, validating the no-equity funding model
risks: Contracting risk — no signed AI demand; talks may not convert, leaving large MW on mining-only economics
BTC price — drives treasury value, mining cash flow and the self-funding buildout plan simultaneously
Speculative-build / financing risk — multi-GW TX capex committed ahead of demand; dilution if project debt requires a signed lease that slips
Execution / competency risk — first-time data-center landlord vs. established colo operators chasing the same hyperscalers
companies.finMix: $1.84B of 0% convertible notes (2030 $692M + 2032 $1.15B); all BTC-backed credit lines repaid with convert proceeds; net debt ~$1.56B excl. BTC. Buildout to be financed primarily by project/high-yield debt against future IG anchor leases (mgmt cites Cipher-style 6-8% hyperscaler-backed debt) plus monthly BTC production and ~$0.8B liquidity; equity (ATM) deliberately a last resort — zero issuance in 15 months, ~20% share count REDUCED via $463M buyback.
companies.thesis: Cheap vs ~1.1 GW of owned, powered TX/GA land plus a 13.5k-BTC treasury, but the AI-landlord thesis is entirely pre-contract (Meta only a 'frontrunner'). A first signed anchor re-rates it from miner to colo.

companies.name: Bit Digital

basis.plannedRaise: holdco — buildout funded at the WhiteFiber (WYFI) level; minimal BTBT-level equity

companies.narrative: Bit Digital (NASDAQ:BTBT) — a holdco whose dominant asset is a ~70% stake in separately-listed WhiteFiber (WYFI: AI cloud + colo), plus a ~158k-ETH treasury and a small legacy BTC-mining fleet. It trades at a deep sum-of-the-parts discount — the WhiteFiber stake alone is worth ~1.5x BTBT's entire market cap. Valued here as SOTP: live look-through to WhiteFiber + ETH marked live − net debt, not as a developer.
bull: WhiteFiber (WYFI) stake alone ~1.5x BTBT's market cap — deep holdco discount
Live look-through to WhiteFiber AI-cloud/colo growth
Large ETH treasury (live-marked) on a clean balance sheet
bear: Holdco/SOTP discount may persist with no catalyst to close it
Value rides WhiteFiber execution + ETH price
BTBT's 70% could fall as WhiteFiber raises equity
catalysts: WhiteFiber re-rating / value crystallization
ETH price
Stake monetization or distribution
risks: Persistent SOTP discount
WhiteFiber execution
ETH drawdown
BTBT-level dilution
companies.finMix: ~70% WhiteFiber stake + ~158k ETH treasury + small mining; low net debt
companies.thesis: Deep holdco discount — its 70% WhiteFiber stake alone is ~1.5x the whole company's market cap, plus a 158k-ETH treasury. Upside is the SOTP closing and WhiteFiber re-rating; risk is the discount persisting.
